model.label: Gemini 3 Flash
model.description: Google • Fast Gemini model optimized for low-latency reasoning, multimodal inputs, and tool use.

pick.rationale: St. John's enters this matchup with significant momentum after sweeping the Big East regular season and tournament titles under Rick Pitino. While Kansas boasts a top-10 defense and the nation's toughest strength of schedule, their offensive inconsistency and late-season struggles suggest they may struggle to keep pace with the Red Storm's more balanced attack. Despite Kadary Richmond playing through injuries, St. John's superior recent form and slightly higher offensive efficiency give them the edge in what should be a high-level defensive battle.

reasoningStep.title: St. John's vs. Kansas Matchup Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: St. John's and Kansas are nearly identical in advanced metrics, both featuring top-15 defenses. St. John's holds a slight edge in offensive efficiency and enters the game with more momentum following a Big East title sweep.
evidence: St. John's ranks 18th in Torvik and 20th in KenPom with a top-12 adjusted defensive efficiency.
Kansas ranks 22nd in Torvik and 21st in KenPom with a top-10 adjusted defensive efficiency.
St. John's won both the Big East regular season and tournament titles, demonstrating elite form entering the tournament.
Kansas played the #1 strength of schedule but showed vulnerability in the late season, including a significant blowout loss to BYU.

You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.

pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
